originalAlarm.description: Value stored to '__thisopt_814' is never read

sanitizedDescription: Value stored to 'thisopt' is never read
model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __STRICT_ANSI__ () Bool
  true)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __USE_EXTERN_INLINES () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 1a3c8e3d840.c: Dead store of `thisopt` — declared at L9, conditionally assigned at L13 (`thisopt = SSL_PROTOCOL_TLSV1_1` if rand()%2), but never used after assignment. New bug (no ERROR; HAVE_TLSV1_X=F → SSL_PROTOCOL_TLSV1_1 undefined → compile error). Real condition: HAVE_TLSV1_X=T. PC simplifies to !_STDLIB_H AND !__need_malloc_and_calloc (no real feature flags; STRICT_ANSI and EXTERN variants cancel out). The `!_STDLIB_H` is restrictive — not satisfied after stdlib.h is included in real compilation. PC is over-constrained — misses the real HAVE_TLSV1_X=T condition and restricts to non-real library macro states.
